identity.mission: Create a modern, luxurious, and culturally rooted Saudi trivia experience that feels like a prime-time TV game show.
core_principles: Fusion: Traditional Bedouin aesthetics x Modern TV Game Show tension.
Emotion: Every screen must build anticipation (Landing) or tension (Game Board).
Content is King: The Question is the hero. The Team is the star.
No 'Digital Flatness': Use texture, grain, and deep gradients to mimic physical game show sets.

rules: NEVER use pure white (#FFFFFF). Use #FDF6E3 or #FAF3E0.
Backgrounds should generally be Warm Beige (#FDF6E3) for light modes (Landing, Setup).
Game Board and Question screens MUST use Deep Burgundy (#5B0E14) or Dark (#2A0409) backgrounds to create 'Studio' atmosphere.
Text on Dark Backgrounds: Use Golden Sand (#F1E194) or Off-White (#FDF6E3).
Text on Light Backgrounds: Use Deep Burgundy (#5B0E14).

landing_page.structure: Central Hero Text (Game Title) with subtle animated background. 'Start Game' button is the primary focus. Team/Category previews below.
landing_page.background_style: Warm Beige with subtle Islamic geometric pattern overlay (5% opacity).

game_board.structure: Header with Scores. Grid of Categories (Columns) x Points (Rows).
game_board.visuals: Dark Burgundy Background. Cards are 'Tiles' that flip or glow on hover. Used questions dim out.
game_board.typography: Big, Bold Numbers for point values (200, 400, 600).

question_screen.structure: Full screen overlay. Massive text. Timer distinct but not distracting.
question_screen.animation: Animate question text in. 'Reveal Answer' button hidden until needed or subtle.
question_screen.background: Deep Burgundy with radial gradient to focus center.

buttons.primary: bg-primary text-secondary font-bold px-8 py-4 rounded-full shadow-lg hover:scale-105 hover:shadow-glow transition-all duration-300 border-2 border-secondary
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent text-primary border-2 border-primary px-6 py-3 rounded-full hover:bg-primary/10 transition-all

cards.category: aspect-[3/4] bg-gradient-to-br from-primary to-primary-dark text-secondary flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-4 rounded-xl border border-secondary/20 hover:border-secondary transition-all shadow-card cursor-pointer group
cards.question_tile: aspect-video bg-card text-card-foreground flex items-center justify-center text-3xl font-black rounded-lg border-2 border-primary/10 hover:border-primary hover:bg-primary hover:text-secondary transition-all cursor-pointer shadow-sm relative overflow-hidden

inputs.text: bg-white/50 border-2 border-primary/20 focus:border-primary px-4 py-3 rounded-xl text-lg w-full text-right outline-none transition-all placeholder:text-primary/30

timer.style: Circular progress bar. Starts Gold, turns Red at 10s.

background_pattern.usage: Use as a subtle overlay on backgrounds. Set opacity to 0.05 on Light bg, 0.1 on Dark bg. Use mix-blend-mode: overlay.

hero_bg.usage: Optional blurred background for landing page or 'Setup' screens.

icons.style: Stroke width 1.5 or 2. Color depends on background.

card_flip.style: Rotate Y axis. Front face: Point Value. Back face: Category Icon/Name (or vice versa).

universal_guidelines: RTL FIRST: Always ensure `dir='rtl'` is set on the html root or main container.
NO PURE WHITE: Use #FDF6E3 for 'white' backgrounds.
The Game Board must feel 'Physical'. Use shadows and borders to create depth.
Typography: Headings in Cairo (Bold/Black), Body in Cairo (Medium).
Data-Test-ID: All interactive elements MUST have `data-testid` attributes (e.g., `data-testid='start-game-btn'`).
Use Shadcn components but OVERRIDE the default styling to match the Burgundy/Gold theme.
instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup Tailwind config with the custom colors (primary: #5B0E14, secondary: #F1E194, background: #FDF6E3).
2. Install 'Cairo' font from Google Fonts and set as default sans font.
4. Create the 'Game Board' using a CSS Grid (Bento style).
5. Use Framer Motion for the card reveal and page transitions.
6. Mock the data for Questions/Categories initially.
7. Implement the Timer logic on the Question page.
8. Ensure the 'Admin Panel' has a separate, cleaner, denser layout but keeps the color scheme.
